message:
The other day, while tidying up the kitchen, I stumbled upon an old board game we used to play as a family. It got me thinking about how we often overlook the simple joys in life—like gathering around a table and rolling dice instead of scrolling on our phones. 

In the spirit of rediscovering low-tech fun, I decided to host a family game night. But before we dove into the competition, we had a little chat about fire safety. We checked our smoke alarms (which thankfully still worked!) and made sure everyone knew our escape plan just in case. 

It felt good to mix a bit of preparedness with laughter and friendly rivalry. Plus, it was a great reminder that safety can be part of our family fun without being a downer. 

What’s your favorite low-tech family activity? ☺️
